perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Control" or "Controlled" means, with respect to Intellectual Property, that SpinCo or a member of the SpinCo Group owns such Intellectual Property, in whole or in part, and/or has the right to grant a license to Parent with respect to such Intellectual Property as set forth herein and without incurring any financial or other obligations to any other Person, subject, in each case, to the terms of any license or other agreement to which SpinCo or any of the SpinCo Group is a party that relates to any such Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Control" or "Controlled" means, with respect to Intellectual Property, that SpinCo or a member of the SpinCo Group owns such Intellectual Property, in whole or in part, and/or has the right to grant a license to Parent with respect to such Intellectual Property as set forth herein, even if such a license would require incurring financial or other obligations to any other Person. This definition supersedes any conflicting terms in other agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Control' to explicitly allow SpinCo to grant licenses even if it incurs financial obligations, contradicting the original requirement of granting licenses 'without incurring any financial or other obligations'. This creates uncertainty in enforcement because the new definition overrides previously specified agreements. Later sections relying on the original 'Control' definition may now be interpreted differently.  The modification to the definition of 'Control' introduces an inconsistency. The original definition expressly states that SpinCo's control over Intellectual Property requires the right to grant licenses to Parent without incurring financial obligations to third parties. However, the modified definition asserts that SpinCo can grant such licenses even if it involves financial obligations, directly contradicting the initial condition. This contradiction creates ambiguity about the limits of SpinCo's control and the financial responsibilities associated with licensing Intellectual Property to Parent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 License Grant to Parent. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, SpinCo hereby grants to each individual member of the Parent Group, on behalf of itself and the other members of the SpinCo Group, and shall cause the other members of the SpinCo Group to grant to each individual member of the Parent Group, a non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual, irrevocable, fully paid-up, royalty-free right and license, for use in the Parent Field, to (i) use, reproduce, distribute, display, perform, make Improvements and exploit the Licensed SpinCo IP, and (ii) make, have made, use, sell, offer to sell and import any goods and services incorporating, embodying or utilizing the Licensed SpinCo IP. The foregoing license shall be transferable or sublicensable by Parent Group solely to a Permitted Party, and, subject to the restrictions herein, with any sale or transfer of a Parent business that utilizes the Licensed SpinCo IP. Any such transfer or sublicense shall require the Permitted Party or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, to agree pursuant to a written agreement to maintain any trade secrets and Information included in the Licensed SpinCo IP in strict confidence. Such agreement shall prohibit any further sublicensing or transfer of rights by the Permitted Party, or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, or any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP outside the scope of the license granted to Parent herein. Parent shall remain responsible and liable for the Permitted Parties' exercise of any rights sublicensed hereunder and any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP by such Permitted Party outside of the permitted scope of the license. Parent shall enforce material breaches of the terms of any such sublicense of rights and notify SpinCo of any material violation thereof by a Permitted Party. If Parent enters an agreement to transfer the license granted to it under this Section 3.1 in connection with any sale or transfer of a Parent business, then SpinCo and members of the SpinCo Group shall be made third party beneficiaries under such transfer agreement to enforce breaches of the license.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Control' now allows SpinCo to grant licenses to Parent even if it incurs financial obligations to third parties. This directly contradicts the original definition, which required such licenses to be granted 'without incurring any financial or other obligations to any other Person'. This new allowance undermines the 'fully paid-up, royalty-free' nature of the license granted to Parent in Section 3.1, creating a direct conflict and rendering the terms of the license agreement uncertain. The original definition limited SpinCo's freedom, but the new definition removes the financial liability restriction, directly impacting obligations under Section 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "Licensed SpinCo IP" means (i) the SpinCo Intellectual Property (excluding Trademark-Related IP), the SpinCo Software, and the SpinCo Technology, and (ii) all rights, interests and claims of either Party or any of the members of its Group as of the Effective Time with respect to Information that is exclusively related to the items of the aforementioned clause (i), in each case subject to the limitations set forth herein, and to the extent Controlled by SpinCo or any member of the SpinCo Group as of the Effective Time (including as a result of the assignments made by this Agreement). Licensed SpinCo IP shall exclude SpinCo Intellectual Property, SpinCo Software, SpinCo Technology, and Intellectual Property: (a) directed to chemically activated carbon products or any processes for manufacturing chemically activated carbon products (including, for the avoidance of doubt, activated carbon sheets); (b) directed to ex-mill processes for purifying crude tall oil and for isolating, purifying and derivatizing lignin from black liquor or any products created using any such processes; (c) licensed to Alberdingk Boley, Inc. ("ABI"), except to the extent outside the "Field," as that term is defined in the "License Agreement" dated February 3, 2006, by and between MeadWestvaco Corporation and ABI; (d) owned by Purification Cellutions, LLC, a joint venture between MeadWestvaco Corporation and Applied Ceramics, Inc.; (e) directed to any products utilizing specialty chemicals derived from co-products of the kraft pulping process sold by SpinCo into the paper or packaging field or any processes for manufacturing such products (including, for the avoidance of doubt, paper sizing); (f) owned by a third party (including for these purposes any joint venture or partnership or similar business entity of which SpinCo is a member or in which SpinCo has an ownership interest) and not sublicensable to Parent or any member of the Parent Group by SpinCo or any member of the SpinCo Group. -2- 1.7 "Other IP" shall mean all Intellectual Property, other than Registrable IP, that is owned by either Party or any member of its Group as of the Effective Time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "Licensed SpinCo IP" means (i) the SpinCo Intellectual Property (excluding Trademark-Related IP), the SpinCo Software, and the SpinCo Technology, and (ii) all rights, interests and claims of either Party or any of the members of its Group as of the Effective Time with respect to Information that is exclusively related to the items of the aforementioned clause (i), in each case subject to the limitations set forth herein, and to the extent Controlled by SpinCo or any member of the SpinCo Group as of the Effective Time (including as a result of the assignments made by this Agreement). Notwithstanding any other provision, Licensed SpinCo IP shall include SpinCo Intellectual Property, SpinCo Software, SpinCo Technology, and Intellectual Property directed to chemically activated carbon products or any processes for manufacturing chemically activated carbon products (including, for the avoidance of doubt, activated carbon sheets). -2- 1.7 "Other IP" shall mean all Intellectual Property, other than Registrable IP, that is owned by either Party or any member of its Group as of the Effective Time.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Licensed SpinCo IP' specifically excludes Intellectual Property related to chemically activated carbon products. The change directly contradicts this exclusion by stating that 'Licensed SpinCo IP shall include' that same category of Intellectual Property. This contradiction introduces ambiguity and creates uncertainty about whether the Parent has a right to use IP related to chemically activated carbon products under the license granted in Section 3.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 License Grant to Parent. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, SpinCo hereby grants to each individual member of the Parent Group, on behalf of itself and the other members of the SpinCo Group, and shall cause the other members of the SpinCo Group to grant to each individual member of the Parent Group, a non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual, irrevocable, fully paid-up, royalty-free right and license, for use in the Parent Field, to (i) use, reproduce, distribute, display, perform, make Improvements and exploit the Licensed SpinCo IP, and (ii) make, have made, use, sell, offer to sell and import any goods and services incorporating, embodying or utilizing the Licensed SpinCo IP. perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes IP related to chemically activated carbon products from the definition of 'Licensed SpinCo IP'. The changed text directly contradicts this exclusion by stating that 'Licensed SpinCo IP shall include' that same category of IP. This creates a direct conflict because Section 3.1 grants Parent a license to 'use, reproduce, distribute, display, perform, make Improvements and exploit the Licensed SpinCo IP'. The contradiction introduces ambiguity about whether Parent has a right to use IP related to chemically activated carbon products under the license granted in Section 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 License Grant to Parent. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, SpinCo hereby grants to each individual member of the Parent Group, on behalf of itself and the other members of the SpinCo Group, and shall cause the other members of the SpinCo Group to grant to each individual member of the Parent Group, a non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual, irrevocable, fully paid-up, royalty-free right and license, for use in the Parent Field, to (i) use, reproduce, distribute, display, perform, make Improvements and exploit the Licensed SpinCo IP, and (ii) make, have made, use, sell, offer to sell and import any goods and services incorporating, embodying or utilizing the Licensed SpinCo IP. The foregoing license shall be transferable or sublicensable by Parent Group solely to a Permitted Party, and, subject to the restrictions herein, with any sale or transfer of a Parent business that utilizes the Licensed SpinCo IP. Any such transfer or sublicense shall require the Permitted Party or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, to agree pursuant to a written agreement to maintain any trade secrets and Information included in the Licensed SpinCo IP in strict confidence. Such agreement shall prohibit any further sublicensing or transfer of rights by the Permitted Party, or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, or any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP outside the scope of the license granted to Parent herein. Parent shall remain responsible and liable for the Permitted Parties' exercise of any rights sublicensed hereunder and any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP by such Permitted Party outside of the permitted scope of the license. Parent shall enforce material breaches of the terms of any such sublicense of rights and notify SpinCo of any material violation thereof by a Permitted Party. If Parent enters an agreement to transfer the license granted to it under this Section 3.1 in connection with any sale or transfer of a Parent business, then SpinCo and members of the SpinCo Group shall be made third party beneficiaries under such transfer agreement to enforce breaches of the license.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 License Grant to Parent. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, SpinCo hereby grants to each individual member of the Parent Group, on behalf of itself and the other members of the SpinCo Group, and shall cause the other members of the SpinCo Group to grant to each individual member of the Parent Group, a non-exclusive, worldwide, temporary license, for use in the Parent Field, to (i) use, reproduce, distribute, display, perform, make Improvements and exploit the Licensed SpinCo IP, and (ii) make, have made, use, sell, offer to sell and import any goods and services incorporating, embodying or utilizing the Licensed SpinCo IP. This license is only for a term of two years, after which it automatically expires, unless both parties mutually agree to extend it in writing. The foregoing license shall be transferable or sublicensable by Parent Group solely to a Permitted Party, and, subject to the restrictions herein, with any sale or transfer of a Parent business that utilizes the Licensed SpinCo IP. Any such transfer or sublicense shall require the Permitted Party or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, to agree pursuant to a written agreement to maintain any trade secrets and Information included in the Licensed SpinCo IP in strict confidence. Such agreement shall prohibit any further sublicensing or transfer of rights by the Permitted Party, or, in the case of a sale or transfer of a Parent business, the transferee, or any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP outside the scope of the license granted to Parent herein. Parent shall remain responsible and liable for the Permitted Parties' exercise of any rights sublicensed hereunder and any use of the Licensed SpinCo IP by such Permitted Party outside of the permitted scope of the license. Parent shall enforce material breaches of the terms of any such sublicense of rights and notify SpinCo of any material violation thereof by a Permitted Party. If Parent enters an agreement to transfer the license granted to it under this Section 3.1 in connection with any sale or transfer of a Parent business, then SpinCo and members of the SpinCo Group shall be made third party beneficiaries under such transfer agreement to enforce breaches of the license.
perturbation.explanation: The original license grant is described as 'perpetual, irrevocable'. The modified version introduces a 'temporary license' with a 'term of two years', directly contradicting the original terms of perpetuity and irrevocability. This contradiction creates significant uncertainty about the duration of the license granted to Parent and impacts Parent's long-term business planning. It directly impacts section 7 where it expresses that the agreement is for a long-term usage and this license being temporary, contradicts the long-term intention of the parties and may cause legal challenges regarding Parent's investments and strategy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Termination. This Agreement may be terminated at any time prior to the Effective Time by Parent, in its sole and absolute discretion, without the approval or consent of any other Person, including SpinCo. After the Effective Time, this Agreement may not be terminated except by an agreement in writing signed by a duly authorized officer of each of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes the license grant from 'perpetual, irrevocable' to 'temporary', creating a direct conflict with the 'Termination' clause (7.1). The original agreement could only be terminated by written agreement of both parties after the Effective Time, implying a long-term agreement. The temporary nature of the license, however, introduces a fixed expiration date, making the termination clause less relevant and creating a contradiction about the agreement's duration. The explanation states that the temporary license contradicts the long-term intention of the parties, and may cause legal challenges regarding Parent's investments and strategy. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition because a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
